description: Identifies avoidable NAT Gateway data processing and cross-AZ routing cost by routing supported AWS service traffic through gateway or interface VPC endpoints when cost-effective and safe.

cost.pricing_source: Static fallback estimate. Prefer AWS Pricing MCP, AWS Pricing API, or cost_report region-specific pricing when available.
cost.note: Gateway endpoints for S3 and DynamoDB have no additional endpoint hourly charge. Interface endpoints have hourly and data processing charges, so model net savings before adding them.

severity_rules.name: s3_traffic_via_nat
severity_rules.condition: same_region_s3_traffic_via_nat_gb > 0 AND (no correct aws_vpc_endpoint for S3 OR missing route_table_association OR endpoint service_name region mismatch)
severity_rules.description: Same-region S3 traffic is using NAT data processing. Add or fix an S3 Gateway VPC Endpoint and associate the private route tables.
severity_rules.recommendation: Confirm same-region S3 traffic and route tables, then add an S3 Gateway Endpoint.

severity_rules.name: dynamodb_traffic_via_nat
severity_rules.condition: same_region_dynamodb_traffic_via_nat_gb > 0 AND (no correct aws_vpc_endpoint for DynamoDB OR missing route_table_association OR endpoint service_name region mismatch)
severity_rules.description: Same-region DynamoDB traffic is using NAT data processing. Add or fix a DynamoDB Gateway VPC Endpoint and associate the private route tables.

severity_rules.name: interface_endpoint_model_candidate
severity_rules.description: High-volume traffic to an AWS service may be cheaper through an interface endpoint, but endpoint hourly, per-GB, AZ placement, security group, and private DNS costs must be modeled.

severity_rules.name: cross_az_nat_routing
severity_rules.description: Private subnet traffic appears to use a NAT gateway in another AZ, potentially adding cross-AZ data transfer cost and reducing AZ fault isolation.

severity_rules.name: nat_hourly_removal_candidate
severity_rules.description: NAT hourly cost may be removable only when all required egress has been safely eliminated or replaced. Requires route and dependency validation.

severity_rules.name: endpoint_misconfiguration
severity_rules.condition: endpoint_exists == true AND (service_region_mismatch == true OR missing_route_table_association == true OR private_dns_misconfigured == true OR endpoint_policy_blocks_required_access == true)
severity_rules.description: An endpoint exists but is ineffective or unsafe due to region, route table, private DNS, policy, or security group configuration.

validation_before_change: Check route tables for every private subnet.
Check service region, endpoint policy, and route table associations.
Use VPC Flow Logs or CUR data to identify destination service mix.
Model interface endpoint hourly and data processing cost before adding.
Do not remove NAT until unsupported internet and third-party egress is verified.
reporting: Separate NAT data processing savings, NAT hourly savings, endpoint net savings, and cross-AZ transfer effects.
Report confidence based on destination attribution and route evidence.
